response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (founding GISCollective, building GIS applications for Green Map) and proven low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic handling millions of requests per second with async I/O and caching. However, his systems-language depth is in DLang rather than the required modern C++ or Rust — his C/C++ experience at National Instruments is roughly a decade old and brief. There is no direct navigation/routing pipeline experience, and recent roles have centered on TypeScript/Node.js product work. Strong engineering fundamentals and adjacent domain exposure make him worth a conversation, but he is not an obvious fit for the core C++ routing-tile requirement.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial data platform experience (GISCollective, Green Map GIS apps)
High-throughput, low-latency systems work (millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, fibers, caching)
Strong craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, and demonstrated full-stack adaptability
concerns: Modern C++/Rust experience is minimal and outdated; primary systems language is DLang
No direct experience with routing engines, map tiling, or navigation guidance systems
Recent years focused on TypeScript/Node.js product and web work, away from systems programming
